person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Louis I (c.? 1304-26 August 1346, ruled 1322-1346) was Count of Flanders, Nevers and Rethel.

«b»Life«/b»
He was the son of Louis I, Count of Nevers, and Joan, Countess of Rethel, and grandson of Robert III of Flanders. He succeeded his father as count of Nevers and his grandfather as count of Flanders in 1322.

In 1320 Louis married Margaret, second daughter of King Philip V of France and Joan II, Countess of Burgundy, who would later inherit her mother's counties of Burgundy and Artois in 1361. This marriage alliance made him break with the anti-French policy of his grandfather Robert III and great-grandfather Guy I.

«b»Count of Flanders«/b»
Louis' pro-French policies and excessive taxations levied by Louis caused an uprising in 1323. Beginning as a series of scattered rural riots, the peasant insurrection escalated into a full-scale rebellion that dominated public affairs in Flanders for nearly five years until 1328. The rebels, led by Nicolaas Zannekin, captured the towns of Nieuwpoort, Veurne, Ieper and Kortrijk. In Kortrijk, Zannekin was able to capture Louis himself. On 30 November 1325 in the church of St. Basil, Louis swore amnesty to the rebels, investigation of John of Namur's actions as regent, and approval of all actions take by regent Robert of Cassel. The following day, Louis was released and fled to Paris.

On 26 April 1326, the King of France, Charles IV, intervened and the Peace of Arques was sealed. This treaty presented the King of France as the aggrieved party, leaving Louis as a bystander. The treaty, not supported by rebel districts in Flanders, did not last long, and soon hostilities erupted again. Philip VI of France to come to Louis' aid, and Zannekin and his adherents were decisively defeated by the French royal army in the Battle of Cassel. Control of Flanders was returned to Louis, with the warning from Philip that if Louis needed rescued again that Flanders would be incorporated into the kingdom of France.

When the Hundred Years War started, Louis remained steadfast in his French policy, even with the county being economically dependent on England. His actions resulted in a boycott of the wool trade imposed by King Edward III of England, which in turn sparked a new insurrection in 1337 under Jacob van Artevelde. In 1339 the count had to flee his lands, never being able to return. Louis was killed at the Battle of Crécy in 1346.

Family
Louis and Margaret had:

1.) Louis II of Flanders (1330-1384), who succeeded him.

He also had a natural son Guy de Rickenbourg.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Louis I de FLANDRES Comte de Flandres, Never et Rethel於1304年出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲時，他的父親Louis I de Dampierre32歲，母親Joan Countess of Rethel24歲。他於1320年7月29日與Marguerite I de BOURGOGNE在法國瑟芭黎絲結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1346年8月26日在法國瑟-吉馬內米奧可雷希-拉-查阿培勒享年42歲，埋葬在比利時維斯吉福拉安德倫布盧潔。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: FLANDRES
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name from Middle English Fla(u)ndres ‘Flanders’, denoting someone from Flanders, a historical region covering parts of modern-day Belgium, France, and the Netherlands. Compare Fleming .

variants.name: Fleming
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from Flanders, from Middle English fleming. The word reflects a Norman French form of Old French flamanc ‘Fleming’, from the stem flam- + the ancient Germanic suffix -ing. In the Middle Ages there was considerable commercial intercourse between England and the Netherlands, particularly in the wool trade, and many Flemish weavers and dyers settled in England. This surname is also common in south and east Scotland and in Ireland, where it is sometimes found in the Gaelicized form Pléamonn.
German: variant of   Flemming  , cognate with 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Louis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hlōd ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’. It was very common in French royal and noble families. Louis I ( 778–840 ) was the son of Charlemagne, who ruled as both King of France and Holy Roman Emperor. Altogether, the name was borne by sixteen kings of France up to the French Revolution, in which Louis XVI perished. Louis XIV, ‘the Sun King’ ( 1638–1715 ), reigned for seventy-two years ( 1643–1715 ), presiding in the middle part of his reign over a period of unparalleled French power and prosperity. In modern times Louis is also found in the English-speaking world (usually pronounced ‘loo-ee’). In Britain the Anglicized form Lewis is rather more common, whereas in America the reverse is true.

variants.name: Lewie
meanings: Anglicized spelling of   Louis  , or a pet form of   Lewis .

variants.name: Louie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Lewie   or   Louis  , currently in fashion.

variants.name: Lewi
meanings: form, since the Middle Ages, of the French name Louis . In modern use it is also in part a transferred use of the surname derived from this given name, or from Anglicized forms of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh ’ or Welsh Llewelyn .

en.lifeSummary: When Louis I de FLANDRES Comte de Flandres, Never et Rethel was born in 1304, in Paris, Île-de-France, France, his father, Louis I de Dampierre, was 32 and his mother, Joan Countess of Rethel, was 24. He married Marguerite I de BOURGOGNE on 29 July 1320, in Paris, Seine, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 26 August 1346, in Crécy-la-Chapelle, Meaux, Seine-et-Marne, France, at the age of 42, and was buried in Brugge, West Flanders, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Louis I de FLANDRES Comte de Flandres, Never et Rethel est né en 1304, à Paris, Île-de-France, France, son père, Louis I de Dampierre, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Joan Countess of Rethel, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Marguerite I de BOURGOGNE le 29 juillet 1320, à Paris, Seine, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 26 août 1346, à Crécy-la-Chapelle, Meaux, Seine-et-Marne, France, à l’âge de 42 ans, et a été enterré à Brugge, Flandre occidentale, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Louis I de FLANDRES Comte de Flandres, Never et Rethel 1304 in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Louis I de Dampierre 32 und seine Mutter Joan Countess of Rethel 24. Er hat am 29. Juli 1320 in Paris, Seine, Frankreich Marguerite I de BOURGOGNE geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 26. August 1346 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Crécy-la-Chapelle, Meaux, Seine-et-Marne, Frankreich und wurde in Brugge, West-Flandern, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Louis I de FLANDRES Comte de Flandres, Never et Rethel è nato il nel 1304 nel Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Louis I de Dampierre, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Joan Countess of Rethel, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Marguerite I de BOURGOGNE il 29 luglio 1320, nel Paris, Seine, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 26 agosto 1346, nel Crécy-la-Chapelle, Meaux, Seine-et-Marne, Francia, all'età di 42 anni, e fu sepolto a Brugge, Fiandra Occidentale, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Louis I de FLANDRES Comte de Flandres, Never et Rethelが1304年にフランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスで生まれた時，父Louis I de Dampierreは32歳，母Joan Countess of Rethelは24歳であった。1320年7月29日にフランスセアインパーイスでMarguerite I de BOURGOGNEと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1346年8月26日にフランスセアイネ-エト-マルンメアウックスクルeシ-ラ-チャペルで42歳で死去し，ベルギーウエスト-vラアンデレンブルグッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Louis I de FLANDRES Comte de Flandres, Never et Rethel(이)가 1304년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Louis I de Dampierre(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Countess of Rethel(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1320년 7월 29일 프랑스 세이인 파리스에서 Marguerite I de BOURGOGNE(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1346년 8월 26일 프랑스 세이인-트-마니 모스 크e시-라-차펠일에서 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 웨스트-블라아더 브루지에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Louis I de FLANDRES Comte de Flandres, Never et Rethel nasceu em 1304, em Paris, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Louis I de Dampierre, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Joan Countess of Rethel, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Marguerite I de BOURGOGNE em 29 de julho de 1320, em Paris, Seine, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 26 de agosto de 1346, em Crécy-la-Chapelle, Meaux, Seine-et-Marne, França, com 42 anos, e foi sepultado em Brugge, Flandres Ocidental, Bélgica.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Louis I de FLANDRES Comte de Flandres, Never et Rethel родился в 1304 г. в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Louis I de Dampierre было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Countess of Rethel, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Marguerite I de BOURGOGNE 29 июля 1320 г. в местности пэрис, син, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 26 августа 1346 г. в местности крeси-ла-чапелл, мокс, сине-ет-марн, Франция в возрасте 42 лет и был похоронен в местности бруг, вест-влаандирен, Бельгия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Louis I de FLANDRES Comte de Flandres, Never et Rethel nació en 1304, en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Louis I de Dampierre, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Joan Countess of Rethel, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Marguerite I de BOURGOGNE el 29 de julio de 1320, en Paris, Seine, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 26 de agosto de 1346, en Crécy-la-Chapelle, Meaux, Seine-et-Marne, Francia, a la edad de 42 años, y fue sepultado en Brugge, Flandes Occidental, Bélgica.
